supplementEntry.name: Palmitoylethanolamide
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Six meta-analyses/systematic reviews and 10+ double-blind RCTs across multiple conditions; 2025 comprehensive SR identified 47 RCTs; pooled SMD for pain 1.68 but high heterogeneity (I2=99%) and many industry-funded trials with small sample sizes limit confidence

cognitive: Adjunctive benefit for negative symptoms in schizophrenia
nonCognitive: Chronic pain reduction across neuropathic, musculoskeletal, and nociceptive subtypes
Adjunctive antidepressant effect with rapid onset when combined with SSRIs
Anti-inflammatory activity via mast cell stabilization
Nerve compression pain relief in sciatica and carpal tunnel

mechanismsOfAction: Mast cell stabilization and degranulation inhibition
Endocannabinoid system modulation via indirect CB1/CB2 enhancement
Pro-inflammatory cytokine suppression including TNF-alpha, COX-2, and iNOS

commonSideEffects: No significant adverse effects reported across controlled trials
drugInteractions: No reported drug-drug interactions in clinical trials

effectSizes.chronicPainPooled: SMD 1.68 (95% CI 1.05-2.31, p=0.00001) from meta-analysis of DB-RCTs (Lang-Illievich 2023)
effectSizes.painClinicalResponse: 81% of PEA patients achieved pain score <=3 vs 40.9% controls by day 60 (Paladini 2016)
effectSizes.bdnfIncrease: d=0.62 medium-large effect (Briskey 2024, 700 mg/day for 6 weeks)
effectSizes.kneeOsteoarthritis: Significant WOMAC reduction at 600 mg (p=0.0012) and 300 mg (p=0.0372) vs placebo (Steels 2019)
effectSizes.diabeticNeuropathicPain: Significant BPI-DPN total pain reduction (p<=0.001) at 600 mg/day for 8 weeks (Pickering 2022)
effectSizes.depressionAdjunctive: Significantly greater HAM-D reduction at week 2 (p=0.004) with PEA 600 mg BID + citalopram (Ghazizadeh-Hashemi 2018)
effectSizes.menstrualPain: Approximately 25% pain reduction vs placebo at 2.5 hours with single 300 mg dose (2025 RCT)

qualityMarkers: Micronized or ultramicronized form for bioavailability

keyCitations.title: Palmitoylethanolamide in the Treatment of Chronic Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Double-Blind Randomized Controlled Trials
A randomized controlled trial assessing the safety and efficacy of palmitoylethanolamide for treating diabetic-related peripheral neuropathic pain
Formulated Palmitoylethanolamide Supplementation Improves Parameters of Cognitive Function and BDNF Levels in Young, Healthy Adults: A Randomised Cross-Over Trial
A double-blind randomized placebo controlled study assessing safety, tolerability and efficacy of palmitoylethanolamide for symptoms of knee osteoarthritis
Palmitoylethanolamide, a Special Food for Medical Purposes, in the Treatment of Chronic Pain: A Pooled Data Meta-analysis
Palmitoylethanolamide as adjunctive therapy in major depressive disorder: A double-blind, randomized and placebo-controlled trial

mechanisms.mechanism: PPAR-alpha receptor activation and NF-kB signaling inhibition

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide for the treatment of pain: pharmacokinetics, safety and efficacy
evidence.findings: PEA activates PPAR-alpha leading to downstream inhibition of NF-kB and pro-inflammatory gene transcription. Recommended dose 1200 mg/day. Poor aqueous solubility (logP >5) necessitates micronization or ultramicronization for improved bioavailability.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic data

mechanisms.mechanism: Mast cell stabilization and degranulation inhibition

evidence.title: Mast cells, glia and neuroinflammation: partners in crime?
evidence.findings: PEA down-modulates mast cell activation and degranulation through an ALIA (Autacoid Local Injury Antagonism) mechanism. This reduces release of histamine, TNF-alpha, and other pro-inflammatory mediators from mast cells, dampening neuroinflammation.
evidence.methodology: Mechanistic review of mast cell-glia-neuron interactions

mechanisms.mechanism: Endocannabinoid system modulation via entourage effect

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide, a neutraceutical, in nerve compression syndromes: efficacy and safety in sciatic pain and carpal tunnel syndrome
evidence.findings: PEA indirectly enhances endocannabinoid signaling by inhibiting FAAH-mediated breakdown of anandamide, producing an entourage effect. Effective and safe across 8 clinical trials in nerve compression syndromes including sciatica and carpal tunnel.
evidence.methodology: Review of 8 clinical trials evaluating PEA in nerve compression pain

benefits.specificClaim: Significant reduction in chronic pain across neuropathic, musculoskeletal, and nociceptive subtypes

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide in the Treatment of Chronic Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Double-Blind Randomized Controlled Trials
evidence.studyType: Systematic review and meta-analysis of DB-RCTs
evidence.findings: PEA is effective and well-tolerated for chronic pain. Pooled SMD 1.68 (95% CI 1.05-2.31, p=0.00001). High heterogeneity (I2=99%) reflects diverse pain conditions and dosing protocols.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review and meta-analysis restricted to double-blind RCTs

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide, a Special Food for Medical Purposes, in the Treatment of Chronic Pain: A Pooled Data Meta-analysis
evidence.findings: Kaplan-Meier estimator showed pain score <=3 in 81% of PEA-treated patients vs 40.9% controls by day 60. Effects independent of patient age, gender, or type of chronic pain. No serious adverse events.
evidence.methodology: Pooled meta-analysis of chronic and neuropathic pain studies

benefits.metaAnalysisSupport: Six meta-analyses (2016-2025) consistently support PEA efficacy for pain

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces diabetic peripheral neuropathic pain symptoms

evidence.title: A randomized controlled trial assessing the safety and efficacy of palmitoylethanolamide for treating diabetic-related peripheral neuropathic pain
evidence.findings: 600 mg/day PEA produced significant reduction in BPI-DPN total pain (p<=0.001) and pain interference scores vs placebo. NPSI total and sub-scores also significantly improved. 94% completion rate.

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces knee osteoarthritis symptoms at both 300 mg and 600 mg doses

evidence.title: A double-blind randomized placebo controlled study assessing safety, tolerability and efficacy of palmitoylethanolamide for symptoms of knee osteoarthritis
evidence.findings: Significant WOMAC total score reduction for both 300 mg (p=0.0372) and 600 mg (p=0.0012) PEA groups vs placebo over 8 weeks. Dose-response relationship observed.

benefits.specificClaim: Improves cognitive function and increases BDNF levels in healthy adults
benefits.replicationStatus: Single RCT in young healthy adults

evidence.title: Formulated Palmitoylethanolamide Supplementation Improves Parameters of Cognitive Function and BDNF Levels in Young, Healthy Adults: A Randomised Cross-Over Trial
evidence.findings: Significant increase in serum BDNF (p=0.0057, d=0.62). Fewer memory errors (p=0.0287). 700 mg/day Levagen+ formulation in young healthy adults.

benefits.specificClaim: Adjunctive antidepressant effect with rapid onset when combined with citalopram

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide as adjunctive therapy in major depressive disorder: A double-blind, randomized and placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: PEA 600 mg BID + citalopram produced significantly greater HAM-D reduction at week 2 (8.30 +/- 2.41 vs 5.81 +/- 3.57, p=0.004) and week 4 vs placebo + citalopram. Rapid-onset antidepressant augmentation effect.

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces sleep onset latency and improves waking cognition

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide for sleep disturbance: A double-blind, randomised, placebo-controlled interventional study
evidence.findings: 350 mg/day Levagen+ reduced sleep onset latency and improved cognition on waking at week 8. No significant difference in actigraphy-measured sleep quality or quantity.

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces acute menstrual pain with single-dose administration

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide (Levagen+) for acute menstrual pain: a randomized, crossover, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Single 300 mg PEA dose at pain onset produced significant pain reduction at 1 (p=0.045), 1.5 (p=0.009), 2 (p=0.015), and 2.5 (p=0.039) hours post-dose vs placebo. Approximately 25% pain reduction by 2.5 hours. No difference in adverse events between groups.

benefits.specificClaim: Improves negative symptoms in schizophrenia as adjunct to risperidone

evidence.title: Adjuvant palmitoylethanolamide therapy with risperidone improves negative symptoms in patients with schizophrenia
evidence.findings: PEA 600 mg BID + risperidone significantly improved negative symptoms vs placebo + risperidone over 8 weeks.

safety.safetyAspect: General tolerability across all clinical trials
safety.claim: Excellent safety profile with no serious adverse events across 1366+ patients in controlled trials

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide supplementation for human health: A state-of-the-art systematic review of Randomized Controlled Trials in patient populations
evidence.findings: Across 47 RCTs covering neuropsychiatric (n=15), neurological (n=17), somatic (n=13), and visceral (n=11) conditions, PEA demonstrated consistent safety. Versatile as monotherapy or add-on therapy. No serious adverse events attributed to PEA.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of all available RCTs in patient populations

evidence.title: Efficacy of Palmitoylethanolamide for Pain: A Meta-Analysis
evidence.findings: PEA well tolerated across 10 studies. May be useful for pain management. Heterogeneous study quality noted but safety consistently confirmed.
evidence.methodology: Meta-analysis of RCTs evaluating PEA for pain

dosage.dosageRange: 300-1200 mg/day; micronized or ultramicronized forms recommended for bioavailability
dosage.claim: Dose range established across multiple clinical trials with dose-response evidence

evidence.title: A double-blind randomized placebo controlled study assessing safety, tolerability and efficacy of palmitoylethanolamide for symptoms of knee osteoarthritis
evidence.findings: Both 300 mg and 600 mg daily doses were effective vs placebo, with 600 mg showing stronger effect (p=0.0012 vs p=0.0372). Supports dose-response relationship.
evidence.methodology: Three-arm (300 mg, 600 mg, placebo) double-blind RCT

evidence.title: Palmitoylethanolamide for the treatment of pain: pharmacokinetics, safety and efficacy
evidence.findings: Recommended dose 1200 mg/day based on available evidence. PEA has poor aqueous solubility (logP >5); micronization and ultramicronization significantly improve bioavailability and clinical outcomes.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of pharmacokinetic and efficacy data
